name: insertTableWithData
description: Inserts a table pre-populated with data at a specific index in the document. All cell content is inserted in a single operation. Optionally bolds the first row as a header. Ragged rows are padded with empty cells to match the widest row.

documentId.description: The document ID — the long string between /d/ and /edit in a Google Docs URL.

data.description: A 2D array of strings representing the table contents. Each inner array is one row. Example: [["Name", "Age"], ["Alice", "30"], ["Bob", "25"]]

index.description: 1-based character index within the document body where the table should be inserted. Use readDocument with format='json' to inspect indices.

hasHeaderRow.description: If true, the first row is treated as a header and its text will be bolded.

tabId.description: The ID of the specific tab to insert into. Use listDocumentTabs to get tab IDs. If not specified, inserts into the first tab.
